L1.area: Network request processing and synchronous resource fetching

L2.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where an attacker-controlled URL is interpolated without sanitization into a dynamically constructed script that is executed as a child process, allowing arbitrary server-side code execution.

L3.area: Synchronous fetch script construction and subprocess-based HTTP request utilities
L3.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where an attacker-controlled URL from a remotely loaded script resource is interpolated without sanitization into a dynamically constructed Node.js script that is executed via a synchronous child process call, allowing arbitrary server-side code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The SyncFetchScriptBuilder interpolates a user-controlled URL directly into a JavaScript code string that is executed via child_process.execFileSync(), allowing an attacker to inject and execute arbitrary server-side code through a crafted script tag URL.
